name: Bookmap
description_short: Real-time order flow visualization platform that renders market depth as dynamic heatmaps, revealing liquidity, large orders, and institutional activity across futures, stocks, crypto, and forex.
description_long:
## What Bookmap Is

Bookmap changed the way retail traders see the market. Before Bookmap, reading the order book meant staring at a numerical DOM ladder and trying to mentally track how bids and offers shifted over time. Bookmap replaced that with a visual heatmap — a continuous, color-coded display of the entire order book that scrolls horizontally as time passes, showing exactly where liquidity is sitting, where it's pulling, and where large players are positioning.

This isn't just a different way to display the same information. The heatmap reveals patterns that are invisible on a traditional DOM: spoofing (large orders that appear and disappear to manipulate price), absorption (a large resting order that absorbs selling pressure without moving), iceberg orders (hidden size being filled repeatedly at the same price), and momentum shifts (liquidity suddenly pulling away from one side). For traders who make decisions based on market microstructure, Bookmap provides information that no other chart type can.

## The Company

Bookmap is developed by Bookmap Ltd, a fintech company focused exclusively on order flow visualization technology. The platform has been in development since 2014 and has built a strong reputation in the professional trading community, particularly among futures scalpers, prop firm traders, and institutional training programs. The company maintains an active educational presence through YouTube, webinars, and a knowledge base.

## The Heatmap — Core Product

The heatmap is Bookmap's signature feature and its primary reason for existing. It works by plotting the limit order book on a price-by-time chart where:

- **Color intensity** represents the size of resting orders at each price level — brighter/denser colors mean more contracts/shares
- **The horizontal axis** is time, scrolling continuously as the market moves
- **The vertical axis** is price
- **The current market price** traces a line through the heatmap, showing how price interacts with the visible liquidity landscape

What makes this powerful is the ability to see ORDER BOOK HISTORY — not just what the book looks like right now, but how it has evolved over the last minutes, hours, or the entire session. You can visually see:

- A wall of liquidity at a price level that held as support (price bounced off it)
- Orders being pulled just before price arrives (potential spoofing)
- A large hidden buyer absorbing everything thrown at it (iceberg/absorption)
- Thin areas in the book where price could move rapidly (liquidity gaps)

No traditional chart — candlestick, bar, line — shows any of this information. Bookmap is solving a genuinely different problem.

## Order Flow Indicators

Beyond the raw heatmap, Bookmap includes a suite of order flow indicators that quantify what the heatmap shows visually:

- **Large Lot Tracker** — highlights individual trades above a configurable size threshold, showing where institutions and large traders are executing
- **Volume Dots** — plots volume-at-price as proportionally sized dots on the chart
- **Strength Level** — identifies significant support/resistance levels based on accumulated order book data
- **Cumulative Volume Delta (CVD)** — tracks the running difference between aggressive buying and selling volume
- **Iceberg Detector** — identifies potential hidden orders by detecting repeated fills at the same price level
- **Stops & Icebergs Map** — shows where stop-loss clusters and iceberg orders are likely located

These indicators are add-ons available on the paid tiers, and they represent Bookmap's competitive moat — the combination of visual heatmap + quantitative order flow indicators is more comprehensive than most standalone tools offer.

## Supported Markets & Connectivity

Bookmap supports multiple asset classes through direct exchange and broker connections:

**Futures:**
- Rithmic (primary futures connection — covers all major US futures exchanges)
- CQG
- Trading Technologies (TT)
- Eurex
- Phillip Capital
- Ironbeam
- Optimus Futures

**Stocks:**
- Dxfeed (US equities data)

**Crypto:**
- Binance, Bybit, OKX, Coinbase, Kraken, Deribit, and more
- The crypto connections are free on the Digital (free) tier — this is a strong entry point for crypto traders

**Forex:**
- FXCM
- Dukascopy

The futures connectivity is Bookmap's strongest suit — access via Rithmic and CQG covers virtually every retail futures broker in the US.

## Pricing

Bookmap's pricing structure has evolved into a tiered subscription model:

- **Digital (Free)** — crypto heatmap data, delayed futures/stocks. Genuinely functional for learning and crypto-only traders
- **Digital+** ($39/month) — adds full futures and stock depth data, premium indicators
- **Global** ($69/month) — real-time professional data, all indicators, multibook capability
- **Global+** ($99/month) — everything plus advanced add-ons, API access, and priority support

Bookmap's free tier for crypto is one of the best entry points in the order flow space — you get the real heatmap with live crypto data, not a demo or simulation. This lets traders learn to read the heatmap on real markets before committing to a paid plan for futures.

## Replay & Practice

Bookmap includes a market replay feature that plays back historical heatmap data, allowing traders to practice reading order flow patterns without risking capital. This is particularly valuable because reading a heatmap is a skill that takes time to develop — the patterns (spoofing, absorption, liquidity pulls) need to be seen repeatedly in real market context before they become intuitive.

## Education & Community

Bookmap invests heavily in education. Their YouTube channel (youtube.com/@Bookmap) features regular educational content covering heatmap interpretation, order flow trading strategies, and live market analysis. They also run webinars and maintain an extensive knowledge base. For a platform with a genuine learning curve, this educational investment is important — it helps bridge the gap between installing the software and actually being able to trade with it.

## What Bookmap Does Not Have

- **No automated trading** — Bookmap is a visualization and analysis tool, not an execution platform. You can trade from Bookmap's DOM, but there's no strategy automation or algorithmic execution engine
- **No mobile app** — desktop only (Windows and Mac)
- **No traditional charting** — Bookmap is the heatmap. If you want candlestick charts with RSI and MACD, you'll need a second platform alongside Bookmap
- **No journaling or performance analytics** — pairs well with a dedicated trading journal

## Who Bookmap Is For

**Good fit:**
- Futures scalpers and day traders who trade off the DOM and need to see where liquidity is hiding
- Prop firm traders who need an edge in reading market microstructure
- Crypto traders wanting institutional-quality order flow visualization (free tier covers this)
- Traders learning order flow concepts — the visual heatmap makes abstract concepts concrete
- Anyone frustrated by traditional charts that can't show the order book

**Not the right fit:**
- Swing traders or position traders who hold for days/weeks — the heatmap is most useful on short timeframes
- Traders who need a full charting suite with traditional indicators — Bookmap replaces charts, it doesn't augment them
- Automated/algorithmic traders who need a backtesting and strategy development environment
- Traders on a tight budget who need futures data (the free tier only covers crypto)

## Bottom Line

Bookmap is the best order flow visualization tool available. The heatmap format genuinely reveals market dynamics that are invisible on any traditional chart. The free crypto tier is a rare gift in the trading tool space — it lets you learn real order flow reading without paying anything. For futures traders willing to invest in the paid tiers, Bookmap provides information that can materially improve scalping and day trading decisions. It's not a replacement for charting — it's a completely different category of market analysis tool, and it's the best in that category.
